a:T35f5,<div class="specification">
<p>The rates of change of the area covered by two types of fungi, X and Y, on a particular tree are given by the following equations, where <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="x" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>x</mi>
</math></span> is the area covered by X and <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="y" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>y</mi>
</math></span> is the area covered by Y.</p>
<p><span class="mjpage mjpage__block"><math alttext="\frac{{{\text{d}}x}}{{{\text{d}}t}} = 3x - 2y" display="block" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mrow>
<mtext>d</mtext>
</mrow>
<mi>x</mi>
</mrow>
<mrow>
<mrow>
<mtext>d</mtext>
</mrow>
<mi>t</mi>
</mrow>
</mfrac>
<mo>=</mo>
<mn>3</mn>
<mi>x</mi>
<mo>−<!-- − --></mo>
<mn>2</mn>
<mi>y</mi>
</math></span></p>
<p><span class="mjpage mjpage__block"><math alttext="\frac{{{\text{d}}y}}{{{\text{d}}t}} = 2x - 2y" display="block" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mrow>
<mtext>d</mtext>
</mrow>
<mi>y</mi>
</mrow>
<mrow>

</mrow>

</mrow>
</mfrac>
<mo>=</mo>
<mn>2</mn>
<mi>x</mi>
<mo>−<!-- − --></mo>
<mn>2</mn>
<mi>y</mi>
</math></span></p>
<p>The matrix <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\left( {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}  3&amp;{ - 2} \\   2&amp;{ - 2}  \end{array}} \right)" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mrow>
<mo>(</mo>
<mrow>
<mtable columnspacing="1em" rowspacing="4pt">
<mtr>
<mtd>
<mn>3</mn>
</mtd>
<mtd>
<mrow>
<mo>−<!-- − --></mo>
<mn>2</mn>
</mrow>
</mtd>
</mtr>
<mtr>
<mtd>
<mn>2</mn>
</mtd>
<mtd>
<mrow>
<mo>−<!-- − --></mo>
<mn>2</mn>
</mrow>
</mtd>
</mtr>
</mtable>
</mrow>
<mo>)</mo>
</mrow>
</math></span> has eigenvalues of 2 and −1 with corresponding eigenvectors <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\left( {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}  2 \\   1  \end{array}} \right)" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mrow>
<mo>(</mo>
<mrow>
<mtable columnspacing="1em" rowspacing="4pt">
<mtr>
<mtd>
<mn>2</mn>
</mtd>
</mtr>
<mtr>
<mtd>
<mn>1</mn>
</mtd>
</mtr>
</mtable>
</mrow>
<mo>)</mo>
</mrow>
</math></span> and <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\left( {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}  1 \\   2  \end{array}} \right)" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mrow>
<mo>(</mo>
<mrow>
<mtable columnspacing="1em" rowspacing="4pt">
<mtr>
<mtd>
<mn>1</mn>
</mtd>
</mtr>
<mtr>
<mtd>
<mn>2</mn>
</mtd>
</mtr>
</mtable>
</mrow>
<mo>)</mo>
</mrow>
</math></span>.</p>
<p>Initially <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="x" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>x</mi>
</math></span> = 8 cm<sup>2</sup> and <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="y" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>y</mi>
</math></span> = 10 cm<sup>2</sup>.</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Find the value of <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{{{\text{d}}y}}{{{\text{d}}x}}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mrow>

<mi>x</mi>
</mrow>
</mfrac>
</math></span> when <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="t = 0" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>t</mi>
<mo>=</mo>
<mn>0</mn>
</math></span>.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>On the following axes, sketch a possible trajectory for the growth of the two fungi, making clear any asymptotic behaviour.</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<div class="marks">[4]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>b:T707,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{{{\text{d}}y}}{{{\text{d}}x}} = \frac{{16 - 20}}{{24 - 20}}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">

<p><strong>Note:</strong> Award <em><strong>M1A0</strong></em> if asymptote along <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\left( {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}  1 \\   2  \end{array}} \right)" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">

<p>trajectory begins at (8, 10) with negative gradient   <em><strong> A1A1</strong></em></p>
